description: A designed quote-card built around a black-and-white screenshot of a CNN remote video interview, framed by a yellow border. The top half shows a split-screen: on the left a woman seated at a laptop, identified by a red-and-white chyron 'CNN TECH REPORTER / Clare Duffy' with the red 'CNN' logo in the upper corner; on the right a bespectacled man with a beard speaking from a home kitchen (the interview subject). The bottom half is a black panel with an ICE statement in white text introduced by a large yellow quotation mark: 'CNN's promotion of an ‘ICE spotting’ app is reckless and irresponsible. Advertising an app that basically paints a target on federal law enforcement officers’ backs is sickening. My officers and agents are already facing a 500% increase in assaults, and going on live television to announce an app that lets anyone zero in on their locations is like inviting violence against them with a national megaphone. CNN is willfully endangering the lives of officers who put their lives on the line every day and enabling dangerous criminal aliens to evade U.S. law.' A closing yellow-text rhetorical question reads 'Is this simply reckless ‘journalism’ or overt activism?”' The technique is a media-criticism/attack graphic that reframes a news report as a threat to officers, using a statistic ('500% increase in assaults') and loaded language to delegitimize CNN. Target audience: supporters and the press, intended to rally outrage against media coverage of ICE-tracking tools.
notable_text: CNN's promotion of an 'ICE spotting' app is reckless and irresponsible...My officers and agents are already facing a 500% increase in assaults...CNN is willfully endangering the lives of officers...Is this simply reckless 'journalism' or overt activism?
